---
title: "5.3.2 Keeping data safe from security threats"
subtitle: "Biometrics · P201-P203"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .figure-placeholder { border: 1px dashed #8aa0bf; border-radius: 8px; background: #f4f8ff; color: #40526b; padding: 1.1em 1.3em; margin: 0.8em 0; font-size: 0.9em; text-align: center; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.58em; line-height: 1.0; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.18em 0.26em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)

## Biometrics

### Unique human characteristics

<span class="term">Biometrics</span> (生物识别) can be used in much the same way as passwords as a way of identifying a user.

Biometrics relies on certain unique characteristics of human beings.

Examples include:

::: {.tight-list}
- fingerprint scans
- retina scans
- face recognition
- voice recognition
:::

## Biometric Applications

### Security devices

Biometrics is used in a number of applications as a security device.

For example:

::: {.tight-list}
- some mobile phones use fingerprint matching before they can be operated
- some pharmaceutical companies use face recognition or retina scans to allow entry to secure areas
:::

## Fingerprint Scans

### Matching stored images

Images of fingerprints are compared against previously scanned fingerprint images stored in a database.

If they match, then a user has been correctly recognised.

The system compares patterns of <span class="mark">ridges and valleys</span> that are unique. The accuracy is about 1 in 5000.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.14 Fingerprint scan screenshot placeholder
:::

## Fingerprint Benefits

### Why fingerprints improve security

Fingerprint scanning benefits:

::: {.tight-list}
- fingerprints are unique, so they are difficult to replicate
- other security devices can be lost or stolen
- it would be impossible to sign in for somebody else
- fingerprints cannot be misplaced
:::

## Fingerprint Drawbacks

### Cost and acceptance

Fingerprint scanning drawbacks:

::: {.tight-list}
- relatively expensive to install and set up
- damaged fingers can affect scanning accuracy
- some people may regard biometric devices as an infringement of civil liberties
:::

## Retina Scans

### Blood vessel patterns

<span class="term">Retina scans</span> use infrared light to scan the unique pattern of blood vessels in the retina at the back of the eye.

It is secure since nobody has yet found a way to duplicate the blood vessel patterns.

The accuracy is about <span class="mark">1 in 10 million</span>.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.15 Retina scan screenshot placeholder
:::

## Table 5.3

### Fingerprint and retina scans

::: {.clean-table}
| Biometric technique | Benefits | Drawbacks |
|---|---|---|
| Fingerprint scans | developed technique; easy to use; small storage requirements | intrusive for some people; can make mistakes if skin is dirty or damaged |
| Retina scans | very high accuracy; no known way to replicate a person's retina | intrusive; slow to verify; very expensive to install and set up |
:::

## Table 5.3

### Face and voice recognition

::: {.clean-table}
| Biometric technique | Benefits | Drawbacks |
|---|---|---|
| Face recognition | non-intrusive method; relatively inexpensive technology | affected by lighting, hair, age and glasses |
| Voice recognition | non-intrusive; verification takes less than 5 seconds; relatively inexpensive | voice can be recorded; low accuracy; illness can change a person's voice |
:::

## Biometric Application

### Door security with retina scanner

In the textbook example, a company uses retina scans to permit entry to secure research laboratories.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.16 Security system controlled by retina scanners screenshot placeholder
:::

## Retina Door System

### Data flow

::: {.tight-list}
1. A person stands facing the retina scanner.
2. The scanned data is sent via an <span class="term">ADC</span> to a microprocessor.
3. The microprocessor compares the data with retina scan data stored in a database.
4. If the data matches, a signal turns a light from red to green and unlocks the security door.
5. The door is controlled by a <span class="term">DAC</span> and an actuator.
6. If the data does not match, entry is denied and the light remains red.
:::

## Activity 5.5

### Textbook practice

The textbook asks students to:

::: {.tight-list}
- describe how a retina-scan entry system might change if face recognition was used
- name other biometric devices that could control entry
- describe benefits and drawbacks of voice control as a form of car security
:::

## Classroom Check

### Biometrics

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- biometrics identifies a user using unique human characteristics
- fingerprint scans compare ridges and valleys with stored images
- retina scans use infrared light to scan blood vessel patterns
- benefits and drawbacks differ for fingerprint, retina, face and voice systems
- the retina door system uses scanner, ADC, microprocessor, database, DAC and actuator
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Two-step verification and updates

The next deck explains two-factor checking and automatic software updates.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)
